title: [BUG] Workbook Read Stream adding blank cell at index 0 of emitted row data
author: colinroemer

comments.author: BENLKAMLENourdine
comments.body: same problem here, an empty field (null) is added at the beginning of each row

comments.author: nandakumar-a
pns09
comments.body: Any updates on this one. I am facing this same issue. I have to stream very big xlsx files in nodejs and this creates a problem. I can use shift method and remove the very first index position as the row.values is an array object but I'm guessing, doing so on each and every row value will be very inefficient approach and also time consuming. Please recommend!

comments.author: pns09
comments.body: No luck using shift() method either. The values array starts from index 1 as opposed to index 0. First index position values[0] is not shown in the array what so ever. eg: Here is my row.values array with no values[0] position and hence shift() method doesn't find the element and won't remove. - Another approach I have tried is by assigning the values[0] with some random value so that I can remove it using shift() method eg : assigned values[0] = 'remove' and then use values.shift() but that is not removing the first element either

comments.author: pns09
comments.body: Okay ......so I copied the row.values array and then used the shift() method on to make it work. I know it is a very ugly approach. Could we please have a fix in place? -Thanks!

comments.author: maaaaagda
comments.body: After debugging it, it seems that the issue is in `row.values()` implementation, or indexing the columns. Columns are indexed from 1, not 0, and this is why we have this result. See the code: https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/ddab279a882aba8f18fc5127c6d59e3aa9f596c7/lib/doc/row.js#L170 I didnt bother with changing the way they're indexing columns, maybe there was a reason for it 🤷‍♀️ . My very basic patch looks like that. I'll see if it works for different excel files. Other option would be to do the below, but I dont know if the order will be fine. `values.push(cell.value);`

comments.author: alfaz151
comments.body:
> ## 🐛 Bug Report
> When using WorkbookReader with a stream, every row that is emitted has an extra index added. for example, looking at the code below, when the XLSX file that is being read has a row looking like below:
> 
> row 1: ['col a' header', 'col b' header', 'col c' header']
> 
> when I console.log(row.values), im getting [,'col a' header', 'col b' header', 'col c' header'] which has an extra comma at the beginning. I have not been able to determine why this is occurring. any help would be appreciated
> 
> Lib version: 4.1.0
> 
> ## Steps To Reproduce
> ```js
> const workbookReader = new ExcelJS.stream.xlsx.WorkbookReader(incomingStream, {});
> 
>  workbookReader.on('worksheet', (worksheet) => {
>       worksheet.on('row', (row) => {
>         console.log(row.values); // outcome is [,'col a' header', 'col b' header', 'col c' header'] but should be ['col a' header', 'col b' header', 'col c' header']
>       });
>     });
> ```


Here the we can use 
`row._cells` to get the correct row array without any blank cell
